threat_model: RepositoryConnection read models can become an authority bridge if provider repository identity, installation status, permission scopes, credential binding, revocation status, and verification time are incomplete or if write-like scopes are treated as executable authority. The mitigation is a reference-only durable contract and validator checks that require revocation evidence, deny raw credential serialization, and keep write authority false.

required_checks.check: RepositoryConnection write authority remains false and write-like effective scopes are rejected
Read-model RepositoryConnection write authority remains false and read_only remains true
redaction check confirms credential binding is reference-only and secret_values_serialized remains false
IDOR check confirms RepositoryConnection remains project scoped and project repository refs remain exact
receipt integrity check confirms projection preserves durable repository connection fields from source contract into read model
audit visibility check confirms schema and SDLC artifacts validate with strict gates

findings.mitigation: RepositoryConnection validators reject enabled write authority and write-like effective permission scopes while preserving requested action class as planning metadata only.
RepositoryConnection records now require revocation_state and revocation_evidence_ref in both source contracts and read models.

mitigations: deny write authority and write-like effective scopes
require revocation evidence before the model is considered durable
keep all examples in Foundation Mode and non-mutating
do not create dashboard, mutation endpoint, branch workspace, pull request, external adapter integration, deployment, DNS, secret, or destructive-operation authority
